policy: Openly licensed historical flags, banners, arms, or near-period visual evidence. Every entry is a reviewable placeholder, not a claim that a modern national-flag convention existed in 1444.

AKK.note: Open reconstruction of the Aq Qoyunlu emblem; retain as a placeholder pending specialist review.

ARA.note: Royal banner of Aragon, used as the country identity placeholder.

BOH.note: Bohemian lion banner; review the precise 1444 royal context later.

BUR.note: Burgundian saltire tradition; placeholder pending a Valois-Burgundy specialist pass.

BYZ.note: Late Byzantine imperial banner reconstruction close to the 1444 date.

CAS.commons_file: Royal Banner of the Crown of Castille (15th Century Style).svg
CAS.note: Fifteenth-century-style Crown of Castile royal banner.

CRI.commons_file: Flag of the Crimean Khanate (15th century).svg

DAN.note: Dannebrog tradition is medieval; exact proportions are a modern presentation choice.

ENG.note: St George's Cross was established as an English emblem by the late Middle Ages.

FRA.commons_file: Royal Standard of the King of France.svg
FRA.note: Royal fleur-de-lis standard representing the French monarchy.

GRA.commons_file: Royal Standard of Nasrid Dynasty Kingdom of Grenade.svg
GRA.note: Nasrid royal standard used for the Emirate of Granada.

GEN.note: St George's Cross as the medieval Genoese banner.

HAB.note: The Austrian bindenschild is medieval; proportions remain placeholder presentation.

LAN.commons_file: State Flag of the Republic of Florence.svg

LIT.commons_file: Royal banner of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania.svg
LIT.note: Vytis/Pahonia royal banner representing the Grand Duchy.

MAM.note: Derived from a 1413 map depiction; requires later Mamluk specialist review.

MLO.commons_file: Flag of the Duchy of Milan (1450, 2-3 ratio).svg
MLO.note: A 1450 Milanese ducal flag used six years early as a temporary visual placeholder.

MOL.note: Aurochs banner tradition representing medieval Moldavia.

MOS.note: Earlier Muscovite dynastic banner reconstruction; explicitly provisional for 1444.

NAP.note: Neapolitan royal identity placeholder; dynastic quartering needs later date-specific review.

NOV.note: Late-fourteenth-century Novgorod banner reconstruction used near the 1444 date.

ORL.commons_file: Arms of the Dukes of Orléans.svg
ORL.note: Orléans dynastic arms rendered as a shield flag for the French appanage.

PAP.note: Pre-1808 Papal flag tradition used provisionally; the precise 1444 banner requires specialist review.

POR.commons_file: Flag of the Kingdom of Portugal (1385–1485).svg

PRO.note: Provençal arms used as a flag placeholder.

QAR.note: War flag associated with Qara Yusuf, who died in 1420; used as a near-period Qara Qoyunlu placeholder.

RAG.commons_file: St. Blaise - State Flag of the Ragusan Republic.svg
RAG.note: Ragusan state flag; exact 1444 form requires later review.

SCO.note: Royal Banner of Scotland; openly licensed reconstruction.

SER.note: Serbian Despotate flag reconstruction appropriate to the start-era polity.

SIE.commons_file: Stemma della Repubblica di Siena - Simbolo della Repubblica di Siena, Comune di Siena, Capitano del Popolo.svg
SIE.note: Sienese republican balzana used as the civic identity.

SWE.note: Fourteenth-century Swedish royal banner reconstruction used during the Kalmar Union era.

TIM.note: Flag detail from a 1436 Zafarnama manuscript, eight years before the start date.

VEN.commons_file: Flag of Most Serene Republic of Venice.svg
VEN.note: Lion of Saint Mark banner representing the Republic of Venice; exact tail and ornament details remain replaceable.

WAL.note: Wallachian eagle banner tradition; retain as a reviewed placeholder.
